title: Caption
text: Caption Window property that determines whether the form window has a title bar. Valid values: True (default) or False . Value Description True The form is displayed with a title bar. If a value is provided for the Title form property, this value is displayed in the title bar. Otherwise, the Name is displayed. Default if Window Type is Normal , Primary , Secondary , or Dialog . False No title bar is displayed, and the System Menu , Iconize , Maximize , and Close properties are also disabled. Default if Window Type is Contained or Popup . Usage Dynamic? No Dependencies: Window Type , Iconize , Maximize , and Close Description When a form is activated as a popup window, the Caption property is not applied. If the form is instantiated with InheritWinProps=True , the Caption property is applied only if it has been explicitly set to true . Applies To Form Application Shells for Desktop Applications Related Links Title Title displayed in the window title bar or browser tab when the application shell, form, or server page is run. If no title is specified, the object name is displayed. Name Name of the development object, which uniquely identifies it. System Menu (SysMenu) Window property of a form or application shell that determines whether the Windows system menu and buttons to minimize, maximize, and close the window are available for this window. Valid values: True or False. Iconize (CanIconize) Window property that determines whether the user can minimize the form or application window at run time, and enables the Minimize/Restore button in the title bar. Maximize Determines whether the user can maximize and restore the size of the form or application window using the Maximize/Restore button in the title bar. Close (CanClose) [FRM] Window property of a form component that determines whether the user can close the form window by clicking the Close button in the title bar. Was this topic helpful?
